creator: freebsd@dev.thsi.be
text:
(In reply to Alexey Dokuchaev from comment #8)

It is needed to bump all rev-dependencies because the dune port includes OCaml libraries in addition to the dune executable. So any of the rev-dependencies (or any of their own rev-dependencies, etc.) could have linked another ocaml library against one the library provided by dune. If I remember correcly, there is at least one port that uses the xdg library provided by dune. It is easier to bump all ports that trying to pin-point which ports provides an OCaml library that links with one of the dune libraries.

See also this comment for an example on why upgrading a library can break its rev-dependencies: 
https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=282086#c3

Basically, to be on the safe side you need to rebuild all the rev-dependencies
once the output of ocamlobjinfo any_of_the_cma/cmxs file changes. Even then, it might not be enough for people upgrading using a local /usr/ports tree, but at least it would ensure that people using binary packages always gets a working ocaml installation.

BTW: That's the reason for me to delay/bundle many ocaml changes at once: it is too complicated for me to track all the ports that need to be rebuild when i don't know what/when/if something gets committed (as some ports would have to be updated each time one of its dependency gets updated... ).

I'd love to take over the maintainance of some ports if that can help.
